entries.title: Widgets
entries.summary: Widgets are customizable home screen elements that display a clear and actionable view of an app's content or actions. This content can periodically refresh.

entries.title: Canonical widget layouts
entries.summary: Craft effective widget layouts by first identifying your core content. Your layout dictates how information and interactive elements are organized within your widget. Android offers several prebuilt layouts for toolbars, text, list and grid-type widgets to streamline this process.

entries.title: Sizing
entries.summary: Design adaptable Android widgets that scale seamlessly. Use our recommended default sizes as a starting point, and test your layouts across different dimensions to ensure optimal readability and user experience.

entries.title: Retrieve a verified email using digital credentials
entries.summary: This document describes using Credential Manager to get a cryptographically verified email address from a user's device. This process removes the need for your app users to verify their email with one-time passwords (OTPs) or magic links.

entries.title: Authenticate with passwords

entries.title: Gemini AI models
entries.summary: The Gemini Pro and Gemini Flash model families offer Android developers multimodal AI capabilities, running inference in the cloud and processing image, audio, video, and text inputs in Android apps.

entries.title: Gemini Developer API
entries.summary: The *Gemini Developer API* gives you access to Google's Gemini models, letting you build cutting-edge generative AI features into your Android apps---including conversational chat, image generation (with Nano Banana), and text generation based on text, image, audio, and video input.

entries.title: Gemini Live API
entries.summary: For applications that require real-time and low latency voice support, such as chatbots or agentic interactions, the **Gemini Live API** provides an optimized way to stream both input and output for a Gemini model. By using Firebase AI Logic, you can call the Gemini Live API directly from your Andro…

entries.title: Jetpack Compose phases
entries.summary: Like most other UI toolkits, Compose renders a frame through several distinct *phases* . For example, the Android View system has three main phases: measure, layout, and drawing. Compose is very similar but has an important additional phase called *composition* at the start.

entries.title: Where to hoist state
entries.summary: In a Compose application, where you hoist [UI state](https://developer.android.com/topic/architecture/ui-layer#define-ui-state) depends on whether UI logic or business logic requires it. This document lays out these two main scenarios.

entries.title: State and Jetpack Compose
entries.summary: State in an app is any value that can change over time. This is a very broad definition and encompasses everything from a Room database to a variable in a class.

entries.title: App shortcuts overview
entries.summary: As a developer, you can define *shortcuts* to perform specific actions in your app. You can display these shortcuts in a supported launcher or assistant and help your users quickly start common or recommended tasks within your app.

entries.title: Add capabilities to shortcuts
entries.summary: Capabilities in `shortcuts.xml` let you declare the types of actions users can take to launch your app and directly perform a specific task. A capability consists of the name of the action and an `intent` targeting the destination in your app that resolves the user intent.

entries.title: Navigation and the back stack
entries.summary: The `NavController` holds a "back stack" that contains the destinations the user has visited. As the user navigates to screens throughout your app, the `NavController` adds and removes destinations to and from the back stack.

entries.title: Activity destinations
entries.summary: In your navigation graph, a destination can be an activity. While it is best practice to have a single activity in your app, apps often use separate activities for distinct components or screen within an app. Activity destinations can be useful in such cases.

entries.title: Nested graphs
entries.summary: Login flows, wizards, or other subflows within your app are usually best represented as nested navigation graphs. By nesting self-contained subnavigation flows in this way, the main flow of your app's UI is easier to comprehend and manage.

entries.title: Shared ViewModel Recipe
entries.summary: This recipe demonstrates how to share a `ViewModel` between different screens (entries) in Navigation 3 using a custom `NavEntryDecorator`.

entries.title: Navigate to a destination
entries.summary: The Navigation component provides a straightforward and generic way of navigating to a destination. This interface supports a range of contexts and UI frameworks. For example, you can use the Navigation component with Compose, views, fragments, activities, and even custom UI frameworks.

entries.title: Navigate with options

entries.title: Integrate asset delivery (Unity)
entries.summary: When integrating asset delivery, Unity games can access asset packs using Addressables or AssetBundles. Addressables are the more recent and recommended asset delivery solution for games built with Unity 2019.4 or higher, while AssetBundles provide support of asset packs in Unity 2017.4 and 2018.4.

entries.title: Implementation samples for Glimmer Button component
entries.summary: This document introduces a new file containing sample implementations for Glimmer Button components, showcasing various button configurations with and without icons, and different sizes.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer Button component
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides the source code for the `Button` composable function within the Glimmer library, offering developers a reference for creating and customizing buttons in their Android UI using Jetpack Compose.

entries.title: Implementation samples for Glimmer Card component
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides sample code implementations for creating Glimmer Card components using Jetpack Compose, showcasing various card configurations and functionalities, such as titles, subtitles, icons, headers, actions, and long text handling.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer Card component
entries.summary: This documentation introduces the source code for the `Card.kt` component, a versatile UI element used to display grouped information within the Glimmer framework, offering customizable features such as titles, subtitles, icons, and actions.

entries.title: API source code for DepthEffect
entries.summary: This documentation introduces `DepthEffect.kt`, a new file that provides the source code for simulating depth effects using shadows within the AI glasses display, enhancing the visual hierarchy of UI elements.

entries.title: API source code for DepthEffectLevels
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces `DepthEffectLevels.kt`, which defines different levels of depth effects to simulate depth on AI glasses using shadows within Jetpack Compose Glimmer components.  It provides various levels of shadow configurations (level1 to level5) to establish a visual hierarchy.

entries.title: API source code for GoogleSansFlexTypography
entries.summary: This document provides the source code for creating a `Typography` instance using Google Sans Flex font, customized for Jetpack Compose Glimmer within the XR display AI glasses context.

entries.title: API source code for GlimmerTheme
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides the source code for the `GlimmerTheme.kt` file, which is part of the Jetpack Compose Glimmer theme, allowing visual customization of Android applications. It explains how to implement and use the Glimmer theme, including color schemes, typography, and component spacing.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer Icon component
entries.summary: This documentation introduces the `Icon.kt` file, which defines the `Icon` composable function and related components for displaying icons with customization options such as tinting and size, and provides context for how to use icons within the Glimmer theme.

entries.title: API source code for Icon Button for Glimmer Icon Button component
entries.summary: This new documentation page introduces the `IconButton` composable function, providing source code for creating icon buttons within the Glimmer framework for Android, specifically targeting use cases in XR applications.

entries.title: API source code for Icon Sizes for Glimmer Icon component
entries.summary: This documentation introduces `IconSizes.kt`, which defines the sizes for icons within a Glimmer component.  It provides the source code for creating and customizing icon sizes in an Android application.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer List component
entries.summary: This new file provides the source code for the `VerticalList` composable, a custom scrolling list component designed for Jetpack Compose Glimmer applications, offering features like focus-aware navigation, visual scrim edge effects, and snap behavior.

entries.title: API source code for List Item for Glimmer List component
entries.summary: This documentation introduces a new file containing the source code for the `ListItem` composable, which is used to represent individual items within a `VerticalList` in the Glimmer UI library. It provides the implementation details of the `ListItem` composable, including its different overloads (clickable and non-clickable), parameters, and usage examples, detailing its role in constructing list-based UI elements.

entries.title: API source code for List State for Glimmer List component
entries.summary: This document introduces `ListState.kt`, which provides the source code for creating a state object that controls and observes scrolling behavior within Glimmer List components in an Android environment, enabling developers to manage scroll positions and interactions.

entries.title: Material Foundation HCT TypeScript file
entries.summary: This document introduces a new file containing the source code for the HCT (Hue, Chroma, Tone) color system in TypeScript. The code provides a perceptually accurate color measurement system that can render colors accurately in different lighting environments.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer projected context
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides the source code for `ProjectedContext.kt`, a helper class for accessing a Projected device's context and features, including launching activities and checking device connection status. It's crucial for developers building applications that interact with XR devices like AI glasses.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer Stack component
entries.summary: This new file, `stack-source.md`, provides the source code for the `VerticalStack` composable function, which creates a vertically scrollable layout resembling a deck of cards, with items overlapping in a three-dimensional sequence.

entries.title: API source code for Stack Item Scope for Glimmer Stack component
entries.summary: This new documentation introduces `StackItemScope`, a receiver scope for managing item decorations within a `VerticalStack` composable in the Glimmer library, enabling features like depth effects and masking for individual stack items.

entries.title: API source code for Stack State for Glimmer Stack component
entries.summary: This new documentation page provides the source code for `StackState.kt`, which defines the state management for a `VerticalStack` composable in Android's Jetpack Compose for XR. This is important for handling the stack's behavior and user interactions within the XR environment.

entries.title: API source code for Surface
entries.summary: This documentation introduces the `Surface.kt` file, which defines the `surface` composable function. It provides a foundational building block for UI elements, handling aspects like clipping, borders, backgrounds, depth effects, and interaction states within the Glimmer framework.

entries.title: Implementation samples for Glimmer Title Chip component
entries.summary: This document introduces a new file containing sample implementations for creating Glimmer Title Chip components. It provides code examples in Kotlin for different Title Chip variations, including those with icons and associated cards, to aid developers in their implementation.

entries.title: API source code for Glimmer Title Chip component
entries.summary: This new file provides the source code for the `TitleChip` composable in the Glimmer library, a component used to display brief contextual information, such as titles or statuses, within a UI built with Jetpack Compose.

entries.title: Foreground services in Android 11
entries.summary: The documentation updates the URL and source metadata, and clarifies the requirements for foreground services in Android 11. Specifically, it specifies that apps targeting Android 11 or higher must declare the 'camera' and 'microphone' foreground service types when accessing the camera or microphone. It also reiterates restrictions on camera, microphone, and location access for foreground services started in the background. These changes matter because they clarify the conditions under which apps can utilize foreground services to access sensitive hardware, enhancing user privacy.

entries.title: Android Gradle plugin 9.2.0 (April 2026)
entries.summary: AGP 9.2.0 includes several R8 changes.  These changes focus on stricter handling of runtime invisible annotations to align with D8 behavior and enhance rule-based matching.  Specifically, it modifies how `-keepattributes` interacts with runtime invisible annotations and introduces support for negated member names in rules, improving control over code optimization and obfuscation.

entries.title: Control and advertise playback using a MediaSession
entries.summary: The `onConnect` method in the `MediaSession.Callback` has been replaced with `onConnectAsync`. The implementation now returns a `ListenableFuture<ConnectionResult>` instead of a `ConnectionResult`, and the `AcceptedResultBuilder` is wrapped inside a `Futures.immediateFuture` call. This change is necessary to allow for asynchronous operations during connection establishment. It ensures that the connection process doesn't block the main thread and improves the responsiveness of the media session.

entries.title: Configure your build

entries.title: Android Gradle plugin 9.2.0 (April 2026)
entries.summary: AGP 9.2.0-alpha07 introduces experimental support for unified test and coverage reports. This feature generates HTML dashboards that consolidate test results and coverage data from different test types, modules, and build variants, providing a comprehensive overview. To enable this experimental feature, set `android.experimental.reportAggregationSupport=true` in your `gradle.properties` file. This change helps developers get a more complete view of their testing and coverage across an entire project, making it easier to identify areas needing attention and improve code quality.

entries.title: Layouts and navigation patterns
entries.summary: Minor revisions were made to the documentation, primarily involving formatting adjustments and the removal of the "Figure" prefix from an image caption. These changes do not introduce new content or significantly alter the guidance provided.

entries.title: Using Compose in Views
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify the use of `ComposeView` within Fragments, specifically differentiating between hybrid View/Compose apps and Compose-only applications. It emphasizes the use of `ComposeView` for migration in hybrid apps while recommending a single Activity and Navigation 3 for Compose-only apps, avoiding Fragments in the latter case. This change matters because it guides developers toward the appropriate architecture based on their project's technology stack, providing clearer direction for integrating Compose UI.

entries.title: Using Views in Compose
entries.summary: The documentation for integrating Views and Fragments within Compose has been updated. The changes emphasize that `AndroidView`, `AndroidViewBinding`, and `AndroidFragment` should primarily be used as transitional tools during incremental migration to Compose. The updates encourage rewriting custom Views in Compose and adopting a Compose-only architecture for new applications. These changes aim to guide developers toward a more modern and Compose-native approach to UI development, promoting better maintainability and performance.

entries.title: Migrate Jetpack Navigation to Navigation Compose
entries.summary: The documentation clarifies that using Navigation Compose isn't mandatory in Compose-only apps, allowing continued use of Fragment-based Navigation for composable content hosting. It also emphasizes the long-term goal of replacing Fragments entirely with screen-level composables managed by Navigation Compose. Additionally, the text refines the explanation of setting up the NavController, highlighting its role in navigation and back stack management following state hoisting principles.

entries.title: Migration strategy
entries.summary: The documentation for migrating to Compose has been updated to clarify the recommended architecture for Compose-first applications. The previous version stated that using Fragments with Fragment-based navigation and hosting composable content was acceptable, but now the document recommends that the end goal is to replace Fragments with Navigation Compose.

entries.title: About subscriptions
entries.summary: Added a note clarifying the requirement for an Activity instance when displaying in-app messages. The note emphasizes that the Activity must have a valid window token and be in a state where its window is created and attached to the WindowManager. This change ensures the Play Store can correctly display the message overlay.

The documentation for the `pathPattern` attribute in the Android manifest has been significantly expanded to clarify how wildcards work and provide important usage considerations. The update explains how the dot (`.`), asterisk (`*`), and dot-asterisk (`.*`) wildcards function, including examples of what they match and do not match. Critically, the changes highlight the limitations of `pathPattern`, detailing the impact of backtracking and the behavior of greedy and lazy wildcards. It also provides best practices to avoid common pitfalls, such as avoiding patterns like `a*a` and recommending `android:pathPrefix` for prefix matching. These changes matter because they provide developers with more detailed information and guidance to correctly use `pathPattern` and avoid unexpected behavior when defining intent filters.

The documentation for `<uri-relative-filter-group>` has been updated to clarify its behavior, usage, and limitations. A critical note was added indicating that this feature is only supported on Android 15 (API level 35) and higher.  Additionally, there are minor text and formatting improvements, including the addition of `https://` prefixes for links and general clarifications throughout the examples.

entries.title: Behavior changes: Apps targeting Android 17 or higher
entries.summary: This document introduces several behavior changes in Android 17. These include stricter background audio restrictions for apps targeting API level 37, impacting how background audio playback and focus requests are handled, requiring foreground services with specific capabilities. It also describes a change to Bluetooth RFCOMM socket behavior, where `read()` now returns `-1` when the socket is closed, aligning with standard Java `InputStream` behavior and potentially affecting apps that rely on `IOException` for connection termination.

entries.title: Behavior changes: all apps
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces app memory limits based on device RAM, aiming for a more stable environment. It also discusses leak detection, integration with Android Studio's LeakCanary, and provides details on how to identify if your app is affected via ApplicationExitInfo and trigger-based profiling.

entries.title: Background audio hardening
entries.summary: This document describes changes to background audio restrictions in Android 17. The update clarifies the requirements for background audio playback, audio focus requests, and volume changes, emphasizing the need for a visible activity or a foreground service that is not a SHORT_SERVICE. It also introduces more specific requirements for apps targeting API level 37 and highlights the conditions under which the WIU requirement is waived for apps using the exact alarm permission. The document explains why these changes were introduced and how to test for compliance.

entries.title: Features and APIs
entries.summary: Android 17 introduces a new anomaly detection service integrated with ProfilingManager. This allows developers to receive profiling artifacts triggered by system-detected performance anomalies, such as excessive binder calls and memory usage. The new `TRIGGER_TYPE_ANOMALY` trigger provides developers with heap dumps for memory issues and stack sampling profiles for binder transactions, enabling proactive debugging before system-imposed enforcements like app termination.

entries.title: The First Beta of Android 17

entries.title: Under the hood: Android 17's lock-free MessageQueue
entries.summary: This document details improvements to the Android `MessageQueue` in Android 17. The changes focus on lock-free programming using atomic operations and branch-free programming to improve performance and reduce contention. This includes using Treiber stacks, LSE instructions, and branchless logic in the comparison of messages. The document also describes the debugging tools built to analyze the changes.

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR
entries.summary: The documentation for XR_ANDROID_depth_texture has been updated to include information on depth map generation, permissions, and runtime behavior. Specifically, the extension now details how depth maps are generated, the need for the SCENE_UNDERSTANDING_FINE permission, and the runtime's responsibility to maintain consistent depth map resolutions throughout the session's lifetime. Also, added a ratification status. Furthermore, added a description of the valid usage and return codes. Finally, added a new enumerant description for XR_DEPTH_CAMERA_RESOLUTION_MAX_ENUM_ANDROID .

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR
entries.summary: This documentation describes the `XR_ANDROID_enumerate_system_extension_properties` extension, which allows applications to query the system for supported OpenXR extensions and their availability based on the current hardware configuration.  The extension provides structures and a function to enumerate extension properties and handle system property change events.

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR
entries.summary: This document describes the XR_ANDROID_eye_tracking OpenXR extension, which enables applications to access eye tracking data on Android devices. It provides information on how to obtain eye positions, orientations, and eye tracking status in both coarse and fine modes, and highlights the importance of user permissions and privacy regarding eye tracking data.

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR
entries.summary: This extension enhances the basic light estimation extension by providing cubemap lighting estimates, delivering more detailed information about the lighting in the physical environment. entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

This enables applications to adapt their rendering resolution dynamically for optimal performance and visual quality.

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR

entries.title: Android XR for OpenXR
entries.summary: This new extension, XR_ANDROID_spatial_component_subsumed_by, builds upon XR_EXT_spatial_entity and provides a mechanism to identify and filter spatial entities, particularly planes, that have been determined to be the same by the runtime.

entries.title: Elevating media playback: Introducing preloading with Media3 - Part 1
entries.summary: This blog post introduces preloading with Media3 for Android media playback. It explains the benefits of preloading, such as faster playback start and reduced buffering, and provides an overview of the two primary APIs for preloading: PreloadConfiguration for playlists and DefaultPreloadManager for dynamic UIs. The post guides developers through setting up and using DefaultPreloadManager, including configuring duration, ranking, and managing media items, culminating in improving the user experience with faster load times.

entries.title: Jetpack WindowManager 1.5 is stable
entries.summary: This blog post announces the stable release of Jetpack WindowManager 1.5. This version introduces new window size classes (Large and Extra-large) for larger screens, like those used with connected displays, and provides improvements such as automatic state saving for activity embedding and the ability to calculate WindowMetrics from the Application context. These enhancements are aimed at making it easier to build adaptive UIs that work well on all Android devices, including those with external displays.

entries.title: Prepare your app for the resizability and orientation changes in Android 17
entries.summary: This document describes the changes related to orientation and resizability in Android 17. The primary change is the removal of the developer opt-out for orientation and resizability restrictions on large screen devices (sw > 600 dp). This means apps must be able to adapt to various display sizes when targeting API level 37. The document also provides guidance on ensuring camera compatibility, suggesting solutions such as using Jetpack CameraX, CameraViewfinder, or manual Camera2 implementation to handle potential issues with camera previews on different devices.

entries.title: RC Releases
entries.summary: This document was updated to include the release candidate versions for several Compose libraries and other AndroidX components. Specifically, it lists the 1.11.0-rc01 versions for Compose Animation, Foundation, Material, Runtime, and UI, alongside rc01 versions for Core Uwb Uwb-rxjava3, Navigationevent, Tv-foundation, and Tv-material. This update provides developers with the latest release candidate versions for testing and integration.

entries.title: Android latest updates
entries.summary: This update modifies the `latest-updates.md` file, incorporating updates for various AndroidX libraries and tools.  It includes new library versions, release dates, and links to relevant documentation, as well as changes in the experimental features section. These updates provide developers with the latest stable and preview versions of libraries and tools for Android development.

entries.title: Analytics
entries.summary: The documentation updates include removing redundant links to the sample code and correcting typos in the Java and Kotlin code snippets related to analytics within the ExoPlayer media library. These changes improve readability and clarity by removing unnecessary references and ensuring the code examples are accurate.

entries.title: Getting started
entries.summary: The documentation for the ExoPlayer 'Hello World' example has been updated. The change modifies the URL in the code example that links to the source code for binding the player to the view. This update is important to ensure that the documentation points to the correct and current source code location.

entries.title: Playlists
entries.summary: The documentation for playlists has been updated to reflect changes in the source code example link. The link in the Kotlin code sample has been updated to point to a different commit in the repository.

entries.title: Manage and play content
entries.summary: The git diff updates the link to the sample code in PreloadManagerManagePlay.kt to reflect the latest changes in the repository. This ensures users are directed to the most current version of the sample code for managing media preloading.

entries.title: Core Compose
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to reflect changes in the composable functions. The specific changes involve updating the links to reference the correct composable functions within the Android Media3 library, ensuring accurate documentation of the available components.

entries.title: Material3 Compose
entries.summary: The documentation for compose-material3 has been updated to reflect changes in the composable functions.  Specifically, the signature of several composables such as `PlayPauseButton`, `SeekBackButton`, `SeekForwardButton`, `NextButton`, `PreviousButton`, `RepeatButton`, `ShuffleButton`, `MuteButton`, `PositionAndDurationText`, `PositionText`, `DurationText`, `RemainingDurationText`, and `ProgressSlider` now include `IconButtonColors` or other parameters like `androidx.compose.ui.graphics.Color` and  `androidx.compose.foundation.interaction.MutableInteractionSource` which allow for more granular control over their appearance and behavior.

entries.title: Native MIDI API
entries.summary: The code examples for the JNI function calls in the midi.md documentation were corrected to fix potential errors and improve readability. Specifically, there were corrections to object and method retrieval, array allocation and population, and calling the Java callback function.

entries.title: Audit access to data
entries.summary: The documentation updates the example code for the `attribution` tag in the Android manifest. The change fixes a syntax error related to the `android:label` attribute, ensuring that the XML is correctly formatted and parsable, which is essential for developers to correctly implement data access attributions.

entries.title: Permission-based access control to exported components
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to fix a typo and improve the formatting of the XML code examples. The change addresses a typo in the `android:exported` attribute and corrects formatting in the XML code blocks for clarity. This ensures the code examples are accurate and easier to understand for developers, thus improving the overall quality and readability of the documentation.

entries.title: Sensitive Data Stored in External Storage
entries.summary: The example code in the documentation was updated to fix a formatting error in the Kotlin code, and to replace HTML encoded characters with regular characters for improved readability.

entries.title: Emulator download archives
entries.summary: The `emulator_archive.md` file was updated with a new iframe source URL. The change updates the embedded content, likely to reflect changes in the emulator archive or related information.

entries.title: Android Studio Nightly
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the new nightly builds, including updated version numbers, codenames ('Quail 1' replacing 'Panda 4'), and download links, while also updating the build dates.

entries.title: Release notes for Android Studio preview
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the change in the Android Studio Panda 4 release channel from Canary to RC. Additionally, several features related to AI, Compose UI testing, and LeakCanary integration, which were previously described, were removed. The Google One integration for Gemini is still mentioned.

entries.title: Android Emulator 36 Closed Issues
entries.summary: The documentation introduces information about the Android Emulator 36.6.4 Canary release, indicating minor fixes and improvements. It provides a brief overview of the updates in the emulator version.

entries.title: Add multi-density vector graphics
entries.summary: The documentation for Vector Asset Studio has been updated with several enhancements, including a video link, improved formatting, and updated references to Android Support Library and API levels. The changes also include clarifications on supported vector graphic types, considerations for SVG and PSD files, and backward-compatibility solutions for older Android versions.

entries.title: Guide to app architecture
entries.summary: This document introduces changes to the Android app architecture documentation. The modifications include refinements in wording, clarifications on app component behavior, and updates to the references to the UI layer and navigation. The document also provides a better understanding of how UI layers should handle state and data, ensuring stability and a better user experience.

entries.title: Display paged lists
entries.summary: The documentation for the `ConcertAdapter` class has been updated to reference the correct URL for the diffing callback implementation. The URL in the `companion object` and the Java implementation were both updated to point to the correct anchor on the developer.android.com website. This ensures that developers can easily find the proper guidance for implementing the diffing callback.

entries.title: Saved State module for ViewModel
entries.summary: The documentation has been updated to correct typos in the code snippets. Specifically, it corrected instances of `getLi<veDataString>` to `getLiveData<String>`, `Li<veDataString>` to `LiveData<String>`, `getSta<teFlowString>` to `getStateFlow<String>`, and `tempFil<eBundle` to `get<Bundle>`. These changes ensure that the code examples are accurate and reflect the correct method names used in the Android framework.

entries.title: Excessive wakeups
entries.summary: The documentation for excessive wakeups has been updated.  The changes include updating links to the new developer.android.com domain, minor text adjustments for clarity, and streamlining the formatting for improved readability. It also provides clearer guidance on the usage of AlarmManager and the use of WorkManager as a modern alternative.

entries.title: Verify App Links
entries.summary: The code snippets in the documentation have been updated to fix minor syntax errors. Specifically, the code now correctly uses the parameterized types for lists and maps, replacing the original incorrect usages (e.g., `ListString` and `MapS>tring, Integer`) with the proper `List<String>` and `Map<String, Integer>` format. These changes ensure the code samples are syntactically correct and can be easily copied and implemented by developers.

entries.title: Android for Cars overview
entries.summary: The documentation for Android Auto and Google Built-in has been updated. The "Google Built-in" link has been updated to "cars with Google built-in" for clarity.

entries.title: Android Developers
entries.summary: This document provides style guidelines for Android car-related documentation, specifying the correct terminology for platforms, libraries, and Google Play services to ensure clarity and consistency.

entries.title: Add support for Android Automotive OS to your templated app
entries.summary: This update revises the documentation for Android Automotive OS app development. Key changes include correcting manifest entries, refining code snippets, and clarifying the implementation of intent handling within the app's session. Additional updates include reformatting, clarifying information regarding package names, and improving the guidance on offline content considerations. These changes provide more accurate code examples, improve clarity, and give better guidance for Android Automotive OS app developers.

entries.title: Build an internet of things app
entries.summary: The example code in the documentation for IoT apps has been updated to use the `CarIcon.Builder` for setting the image, and a `Header.Builder` for the title with an app icon. This change ensures that the image is correctly rendered within the car's UI and that the header is properly formatted. This is important for consistency and correct display on the car's infotainment system.

entries.title: Create your start screen
entries.summary: The example code for creating a start screen using a PaneTemplate has been updated. The code now uses the `Header.Builder` to set the start header action, replacing the previous `setHeaderAction` call. This change improves code clarity and aligns with the current Android Car App Library best practices for screen layout.

entries.title: Build a templated media app
entries.summary: The documentation for media apps has been updated to clarify that templated media apps can be published to internal and closed testing tracks on the Google Play Store, replacing the ambiguous term 'Play Store'. This change ensures accuracy and better understanding for developers.

entries.title: Build a point of interest app
entries.summary: The documentation for POI apps has been updated to clarify the declaration of the car app category and to streamline the code examples.  The change simplifies the code snippets for setting the content refresh listener, removing the language-specific examples (Kotlin/Java) and using a single, more concise example.

entries.title: Car ready mobile apps
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify that if an app relies on a Google Play services API that isn't available on cars with Google built-in, an alternative must be implemented or the dependency removed. This ensures apps are compatible with the automotive platform.

entries.title: Media apps for cars overview
entries.summary: The documentation for the media service has been updated. The reference to `MediaLibrarySerice` has been corrected to point to `MediaLibraryService` and updated to use the correct link for the API for media content serving, changing from Android training to the Media3 session documentation.

entries.title: Build your content hierarchy
entries.summary: The documentation for the Android Auto media browser service has been updated to clarify that Google Assistant utilizes distinct package names for its mobile and Android Automotive OS (AAOS) applications, rather than a single set of package names. This change ensures developers correctly configure package validation to allow Google Assistant access to the media browser service.

entries.title: Handle errors
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to clarify that Android Automotive OS (AAOS) sets the playback state to `STATE_ERROR`, providing localized error messages. This update improves clarity regarding error handling on the AAOS platform.

entries.title: Build parked apps for cars
entries.summary: The documentation clarifies that Google Play Store uses filtering to prevent parked apps from being distributed to devices without necessary fixes to ensure a good user experience. This update provides more context on why a parked app might not be installable on a device, even if it's otherwise compatible, due to Google's collaboration with OEMs.

entries.title: Google Play on Android Automotive OS
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect the Google Play Core libraries interacting with the Google Play app. This change clarifies the scope of the library's functionality within the Android Automotive OS environment.

entries.title: Test Android apps for cars
entries.summary: The documentation for testing Android Automotive OS apps has been updated to reflect the current terminology used by Google Play. The terms "Play Store" and "Internal Test Track" are used instead of "Google Play" and "Internal Test Track" respectively, to improve clarity. This change ensures the documentation remains accurate and consistent with the platform's current language.

entries.title: What's new
entries.summary: This document has been updated to reflect changes in the Google Play services for cars with Google built-in, specifically mentioning that the AppSearch module is available as part of Google Play services for cars with Google built-in. Similar updates were made for the Thread Network, and Play Games Services (v2) modules.

entries.title: Create a stub authenticator
entries.summary: The documentation for creating an authenticator has been updated. The changes include correcting the XML code snippets for the authenticator and its declaration in the manifest by fixing syntax errors, such as closing tags and missing attributes. This ensures the code examples are accurate and functional for developers implementing authentication in their Android applications.

entries.title: Recommendations in Android N and earlier
entries.summary: The changes in the recommendations-row.md documentation involve correcting code snippets in the manifest file. Specifically, it corrected the closing tags for service declaration and receiver declaration. These changes ensure the correct declaration and enablement of background services and broadcast receivers within an Android TV application.

entries.title: Introduce first-time users to your app
entries.summary: The change fixes an issue in the `onboarding.md` documentation by correcting an error in the XML attribute for the theme applied to the Onboarding activity. The previous version contained a typo with extra characters which has been corrected to properly define the theme, ensuring the OnboardingSupportFragment uses the expected style. This is important to ensure the onboarding screen is correctly styled and displayed to the user.

entries.title: Migrate from Material 2.5 to Material 3 in Compose for Wear OS
entries.summary: The provided diff shows a change in the URL pointing to a code snippet in the 'migrate-to-material3.md' document. Specifically, the commit hash in the URL has been updated, indicating a change in the referenced code snippet. This matters because it ensures that the documentation accurately reflects the current state of the code example and prevents any discrepancies between the documentation and the source code.

entries.title: Navigation with Compose for Wear OS
entries.summary: The change updates the URL of the code snippet example from the GitHub repository. It fixes the commit hash in the URL to point to the correct version of the code snippet. This ensures that the documentation references the intended code example.

entries.title: Develop for different screen sizes
entries.summary: The git diff shows a change to the URL of a code snippet within the screen-size.md documentation. The snippet's commit hash has been updated. This change is related to a code repository version change.

entries.title: Display ongoing activities
entries.summary: The example code's link to the AlwaysOnService.kt file has been updated in the documentation. This change likely reflects an update in the code snippet's repository, ensuring the documentation reflects the most current code location and version.

entries.title: Watch Face Push
entries.summary: The provided code snippet for running the validator has been updated with a new link to the source code on GitHub. This change is important because it updates the specific code example used for reference.

entries.title: Define user configurations
entries.summary: The documentation was updated to reflect changes in the Android snippets repository. Specifically, the links to the sample XML files for boolean and list configurations in the watchface snippets were updated to point to the latest commit.
